description: Baseline parity run failed because rubocop exits non-zero when offenses exist; command did not tolerate expected lint exit status.

asi.hypothesis: Measure offense-count parity vs rubocop and warm runtime in one command.
asi.rollback_reason: Experiment command treated expected lint exit code 1 as failure.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun with rubocop/nitrocop exit codes normalized (append `|| true`) and parse JSON output files.

description: Baseline measurement after crash-recovery: nitrocop count 3230 vs rubocop 3153 (delta 77), warm runtime 26.3ms (<100ms) but parity not met.

asi.hypothesis: Establish parity and warm runtime on target repo with preview enabled.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric offense_count_delta is worse than target (77 > 0).
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply syntax-diagnostic dedup fix and add regression tests, then rerun parity measurement.

description: Applied syntax recovery filtering + tests, but parity command still showed delta 77 due stale result-cache reuse; warm runtime stayed 25ms.

asi.hypothesis: Filtering secondary Prism syntax diagnostics should reduce over-count to match RuboCop.
asi.rollback_reason: Measured primary metric unchanged (77) because cached diagnostics masked code change.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun parity check with --no-cache for correctness metric, then separately measure warm cached runtime.
asi.learned: Nitrocop result cache can preserve prior offense totals across binary changes for same files.

description: Implemented syntax-diagnostic recovery filtering (drop secondary '..., ignoring it' at same location when primary exists), added two linter regression tests, and restored rubocop parity: 3153 vs 3153 with warm runtime 25.59ms.

asi.hypothesis: Prism recovery emits secondary syntax diagnostics that RuboCop does not count; suppressing those should recover offense-count parity without runtime regression.

name: nitrocop warm runtime under parity constraint on wt-gph-rspec-rip-out

description: Baseline for warm runtime with parity guard: offense count delta remained 0 and warm cached run measured 26.23ms.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline warm runtime while enforcing rubocop count parity.

description: Reduced default rayon cap from 8 to 4 threads to lower warm overhead, but warm runtime regressed from 26.23ms to 30.44ms (parity stayed 0).

asi.hypothesis: Lower worker count may reduce scheduling overhead for cache-hit workloads.
asi.next_action_hint: Try dynamic cap based on file count/cache-hit ratio instead of fixed lower cap.

description: Tried rayon cap=6; parity remained 0 but warm runtime was 26.57ms, slightly worse than 26.23ms baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A moderate cap (6) might balance overhead and parallelism better than 8.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cap=8 and focus on cache-key invalidation/runtime-path optimizations instead.

description: Exploratory thread-count sweep (via RAYON_NUM_THREADS env) found best warm sample at 8 threads (25.51ms) with parity delta 0, but this matched current default behavior and did not represent a code change.

asi.hypothesis: Check whether alternative rayon worker counts can improve warm runtime while preserving parity.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change was made; observed improvement is likely sampling noise and not a durable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Validate noise floor with repeated default measurements before accepting runtime gains.

description: Repeated default warm measurement with parity guard (delta 0) averaged 27.39ms across 8 samples, indicating prior 25.51ms observation was noise.

asi.hypothesis: Re-run default warm timing to validate whether prior apparent improvement is real.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed vs baseline and contradicted prior run, confirming high measurement noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Use median-of-many samples and avoid tiny deltas (<~1ms) for keep decisions.

description: Measured warm runtime noise and stderr skip-summary impact without code changes: baseline avg 25.33ms vs 25.68ms with --quiet-skips; parity remained 0.

asi.hypothesis: If skip-summary stderr emission is a measurable overhead, suppressing it for machine output might improve warm runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; observed delta is tiny and likely noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue real code-path optimizations (cache key/versioning or execution-path pruning), not CLI-flag variance.

description: Added cache session invalidation salt from current binary signature (path+size+mtime) so result-cache entries are isolated across rebuilds; parity stayed exact (delta 0) and warm runtime measured 25.94ms.

asi.hypothesis: Stale result-cache entries across local rebuilds can mask correctness changes; including binary signature in session hash should preserve parity checks and may also improve warm consistency.

description: Confirmation rerun after cache-signature change measured 26.30ms (delta 0), indicating the prior 25.94ms result is within noise and not a reliable warm-runtime improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the same setup will confirm whether 25.94ms is a stable improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not hold up on confirmation (26.30ms ~ baseline/noise).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the correctness-focused cache-signature commit for stale-cache safety, but seek larger runtime wins before additional keeps.

description: Optimized session-hash construction by avoiding clone+sort when include/exclude vectors are already sorted, but warm runtime regressed to 26.45ms (parity delta stayed 0).

asi.hypothesis: Reducing allocations in compute_session_hash should improve startup/warm runtime.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this micro-optimization and focus on larger hot-path costs outside session-hash setup.

description: Profiling run showed full stat-hit cache behavior (6101 stat hits, 0 misses, debug wall 15ms) but end-to-end warm runtime averaged 26.59ms, no improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Debug phase breakdown may reveal a larger runtime bottleneck to target next.
asi.rollback_reason: No runtime improvement in this profiling-only run.
asi.next_action_hint: Target process startup/config-load overhead rather than lint phases (which are already near-zero on warm cache hits).
